note_data.title: Line Bisectors and Angle Bisectors
keyPoints: Perpendicular bisector of a line segment is a line perpendicular to the segment and passing through its midpoint
Every point on the perpendicular bisector of a line segment is equidistant from the endpoints of the segment
Angle bisector divides an angle into two equal parts
Every point on the bisector of an angle is equidistant from the arms of the angle
The perpendicular from any point on angle bisector to the arms of the angle are equal
Three angle bisectors of a triangle meet at a point called incenter
The incenter is equidistant from all three sides of the triangle
Perpendicular bisectors of the sides of a triangle meet at a point called circumcenter

definitions.definition: A line which is perpendicular to a line segment and passes through its midpoint is called perpendicular bisector of the line segment.
A ray which divides an angle into two equal angles is called angle bisector.
The point of intersection of the three angle bisectors of a triangle is called incenter.
The point of intersection of the perpendicular bisectors of the sides of a triangle is called circumcenter.
A point is said to be equidistant from two points if its distance from both points is equal.

importantQuestions.question: Define perpendicular bisector and state its property.
importantQuestions.answer: A line which is perpendicular to a line segment and passes through its midpoint is called perpendicular bisector. Property: Every point on the perpendicular bisector is equidistant from the endpoints of the segment.

importantQuestions.question: Prove that every point on the perpendicular bisector of a line segment is equidistant from the endpoints of the segment.
importantQuestions.answer: Given: PQ is a line segment, MN is perpendicular bisector intersecting PQ at R. Point A is on MN. To Prove: AP = AQ. Proof: In triangles ARP and ARQ, AR = AR (common), ∠ARP = ∠ARQ = 90°, PR = QR (R is midpoint). Therefore, △ARP ≅ △ARQ (SAS). Hence AP = AQ (corresponding sides).

mcqs.question: The incenter of a triangle is the intersection point of:
If a point lies on the perpendicular bisector of a line segment AB, then it is:
The circumcenter of a triangle is equidistant from:

commonMistakes: Students confuse incenter with circumcenter - remember incenter is for angle bisectors
Forgetting to mark right angles in perpendicular bisector diagrams
Not stating 'given' and 'to prove' clearly in geometry proofs
Confusing equidistant from sides vs equidistant from vertices
Drawing angle bisector incorrectly - it should divide angle into exactly equal parts
examTips: This chapter usually has 1 MCQ about incenter/circumcenter and 1 proof question worth 5-8 marks
Always draw neat diagrams with proper labels - marks are given for correct diagrams
Learn the converse theorems as well - they often appear in long questions
Practice construction of perpendicular bisector and angle bisector with compass and ruler
note_data.keyDiagram: Triangle ABC with three angle bisectors AD, BE, CF meeting at point I (incenter). Show that I is equidistant from all three sides by drawing perpendiculars from I to each side. Mark equal distances clearly.
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to define perpendicular bisector of a line segment and state its properties
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q7 (6 marks) — required construction of angle bisector using compass and straightedge with proper steps
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q4 (5 marks) — asked to prove that any point on perpendicular bisector is equidistant from endpoints
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q6 (4 marks) — definition of angle bisector and its properties with diagram

workedExample.question: Define perpendicular bisector of a line segment. Construct the perpendicular bisector of line segment AB = 6 cm. (6 marks)
steps: Definition: A perpendicular bisector of a line segment is a line that passes through the midpoint of the segment and is perpendicular to it
Draw line segment AB = 6 cm using ruler
With A as center and radius more than half of AB, draw arcs above and below AB
With B as center and same radius, draw arcs to intersect previous arcs at points P and Q
Join P and Q with a straight line using ruler
Line PQ is the perpendicular bisector of AB, meeting AB at point M (midpoint)
workedExample.answer: Perpendicular bisector is a line passing through midpoint of line segment and perpendicular to it. Construction completed showing PQ ⊥ AB at midpoint M.
workedExample.markScheme: 2 marks for correct definition, 4 marks for accurate construction steps with proper arc intersections

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general answers instead of textbook-exact definitions. Always use the STBB textbook phrasing for perpendicular bisector and angle bisector.
In construction questions, marks are deducted for not showing compass arcs clearly or not maintaining proper radius throughout the construction process.
When proving properties, students must state that 'any point on perpendicular bisector is equidistant from endpoints' using exact textbook language, not informal explanations.
note_data.quickRecap: Perpendicular bisector passes through midpoint and is perpendicular to line segment. Angle bisector divides angle into two equal parts. Any point on perpendicular bisector is equidistant from endpoints. Construction uses compass arcs with radius greater than half the segment length.
